title: HGETALL
description: Returns all fields and values in a hash.
complexity: O(N) where N is the size of the hash.

returns.description: All the fields and values contained into a hash.

lettuce_sync.signature: Long hgetall(KeyValueStreamingChannel<K, V> channel, K key)

lettuce_async.signature: RedisFuture<Long> hgetall(KeyValueStreamingChannel<K, V> channel, K key)

returns.description: Long count of the keys. @deprecated since 6.0 in favor of consuming large results through the org.reactivestreams.Publisher returned by #hgetall.
Long count of the keys. @deprecated since 6.0 in favor of consuming large results through the org.reactivestreams.Publisher returned by #hgetall.

lettuce_reactive.signature: Mono<Long> hgetall(KeyValueStreamingChannel<K, V> channel, K key)

params.description: The key of the hash to get all entries from.
params.name: key

params.description: The flags to use for this operation.
params.name: flags

returns.description: List of fields and their values stored in the hash, or an empty list when key does not exist.

params.description: The key of the hash to get all entries from.
params.name: key

params.description: The flags to use for this operation.
params.name: flags

returns.description: List of fields and their values stored in the hash, or an empty list when key does not exist.

returns.description: List of fields and their values stored in the hash, or an empty list when key does not exist.

content:
Returns all fields and values of the hash stored at `key`. In the returned value, every field name is followed by its value, so the length of the reply is twice the size of the hash. ## Examples {{< clients-example set="cmds_hash" step="hgetall" description="Foundational: Retrieve all fields and values from a hash using HGETALL (returns alternating field-value pairs, useful for loading entire hash data)" difficulty="beginner" >}} redis> HSET myhash field1 "Hello" (integer) 1 redis> HSET myhash field2 "World" (integer) 1 redis> HGETALL myhash 1) "field1" 2) "Hello" 3) "field2" 4) "World" {{< /clients-example >}} Give these commands a try in the interactive console: {{% redis-cli %}} HSET myhash field1 "Hello" HSET myhash field2 "World" HGETALL myhash {{% /redis-cli %}} ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="hgetall-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of fields and their values, or an empty list when key does not exist. -tab-sep- [Map reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#maps): a map of fields and their values, or an empty list when key does not exist. {{< /multitabs >}}
example:
redis> HSET myhash field1 "Hello"
(integer) 1
redis> HSET myhash field2 "World"
(integer) 1
redis> HGETALL myhash
1) "field1"
2) "Hello"
3) "field2"
4) "World"
intro:
Returns all fields and values of the hash stored at `key`.
In the returned value, every field name is followed by its value, so the length
of the reply is twice the size of the hash.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Returns all fields and values of the hash stored at `key`.
In the returned value, every field name is followed by its value, so the length
of the reply is twice the size of the hash.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
{{< clients-example set="cmds_hash" step="hgetall" description="Foundational: Retrieve all fields and values from a hash using HGETALL (returns alternating field-value pairs, useful for loading entire hash data)" difficulty="beginner" >}}
redis> HSET myhash field1 "Hello"
(integer) 1
redis> HSET myhash field2 "World"
(integer) 1
redis> HGETALL myhash
1) "field1"
2) "Hello"
3) "field2"
4) "World"
{{< /clients-example >}}

Give these commands a try in the interactive console:

{{% redis-cli %}}
HSET myhash field1 "Hello"
HSET myhash field2 "World"
HGETALL myhash
{{% /redis-cli %}}

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility
sections.content:
| Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | |
